heroSlides.title: Welcome to Amhara Sint Woreda
heroSlides.subtitle: A land of beauty, culture, and progress in South Wollo Zone, Amhara Region

heroSlides.title: Majestic Highland Landscapes
heroSlides.subtitle: Rolling highlands and fertile valleys stretching from the Blue Nile to Mount Tabor at 4,247m

heroSlides.title: Rich Natural Heritage
heroSlides.subtitle: Diverse ecosystems and breathtaking scenery in the heart of the Amhara highlands

heroSlides.title: Vibrant Cultural Traditions
heroSlides.subtitle: Ancient Orthodox heritage, Meskel celebrations, and time-honored customs define our community

heroSlides.title: The Blue Nile Canyon
heroSlides.subtitle: The western boundary of the woreda features one of the most dramatic river canyons in all of Ethiopia

heroSlides.title: Agricultural Heartland
heroSlides.subtitle: Supporting farmers with modern techniques across 1,437 km² of fertile highland terrain

heroSlides.title: Building the Future
heroSlides.subtitle: Roads, schools, and health facilities connecting communities to opportunities

heroSlides.title: Trade & Commerce
heroSlides.subtitle: Local markets, cooperatives, and enterprises driving economic growth

heroSlides.title: Education & Youth Empowerment
heroSlides.subtitle: Amhara Sint TVET College and preparatory schools preparing the next generation

heroSlides.title: Our Vision
heroSlides.subtitle: A prosperous, equitable, and self-reliant Amhara Sint — rooted in tradition, reaching for the future

culturalPlaces.title: Tadbaba Maryam Monastery
culturalPlaces.description: One of the most historically significant monasteries in Ethiopia, founded during the reign of Emperor Menelik I. It houses sacred relics, ancient manuscripts, and royal crowns.
highlights: Founded during Emperor Menelik I's reign
Key site in the Battle of Wayna Daga (1543)

culturalPlaces.title: Borena Sayint National Park
culturalPlaces.description: A stunning park offering a perfect mix of culture and nature with diverse wildlife, dramatic cliffs, and traditional apiculture communities.
highlights: Dramatic cliffs plunging to the Abay canyon
Diverse wildlife unique to the region

culturalPlaces.title: Mount Tabor (Tabor Terara)
culturalPlaces.location: Border with Legambo, ~170 km East of Dessie
culturalPlaces.description: The highest point in the entire South Wollo Zone at 4,247 meters, hosting unique wildlife and profound spiritual significance.
highlights: Highest peak in South Wollo Zone (4,247m)
Unique wildlife endemic to the area

culturalPlaces.title: The Abay (Blue Nile) Canyon
culturalPlaces.description: The western boundary defined by the spectacular canyon of the Abay River with dramatic elevation gradients from 3,000m to 500m.
highlights: Connects to Gojjam region since antiquity

serviceCards.title: Education
serviceCards.desc: 100 schools, 3 preparatory schools, and a TVET college providing quality education.

serviceCards.title: Health
serviceCards.desc: 1 hospital and 15 clinics delivering comprehensive primary healthcare.

serviceCards.title: Agriculture
serviceCards.desc: Supporting farming households with modern techniques across 1,437 km².

serviceCards.title: Trade
serviceCards.desc: Promoting enterprises, market linkages, and cooperative development.

serviceCards.title: Technology
serviceCards.desc: Digital transformation, ICT training, and e-government services.

serviceCards.title: Construction
serviceCards.desc: Roads, public buildings, and community facilities development.

serviceCards.title: Transport
serviceCards.desc: Road networks and connectivity linking all 37 kebeles together.

serviceCards.title: Water
serviceCards.desc: Clean water points and sanitation programs for communities.

serviceCards.title: Justice
serviceCards.desc: Community policing, courts, and dispute resolution services.

serviceCards.title: Finance
serviceCards.desc: Revenue collection, budget management, and financial administration.
